description: Rich interactive TUI chat experience for `hex chat` — ratatui + crossterm, streaming SSE tokens, conversation history, and project context injection

specs.description: User can start an interactive chat session and receive a streaming response
specs.given: hex-nexus is running with at least one registered inference provider
specs.when: `hex chat` is run in a terminal
specs.then: A full-screen ratatui TUI appears; user types a message, presses Enter, and tokens stream into the assistant message area one by one

specs.description: Conversation history is preserved across turns within a session
specs.given: A hex chat session with two prior user+assistant message pairs
specs.then: All prior messages are included in the API request messages array, enabling multi-turn context; prior messages remain visible in the scroll area

specs.description: Streaming SSE endpoint delivers token deltas to the TUI in real time
specs.given: A POST to /api/inference/chat/stream with a valid messages array
specs.when: The upstream provider returns an SSE stream with delta chunks
specs.then: Each chunk arrives as `data: {"token":"..."}` and the final chunk as `data: {"done":true,"model":"...","input_tokens":N,"output_tokens":N}`

specs.description: Token and model counts are displayed in the status bar
specs.given: A completed streaming exchange
specs.then: The status bar updates to show cumulative `input_tokens + output_tokens` and the model name used

specs.description: `--no-tui` flag routes through plain stdout for pipe-friendly usage
specs.given: `hex chat --no-tui --message 'hello'` is called
specs.then: The assistant response is printed to stdout as plain text with no ANSI escape codes and exits with code 0

specs.description: Chat command fails gracefully when nexus is not running
specs.given: hex-nexus daemon is not running
specs.then: The command prints a user-friendly error `Cannot reach hex-nexus — run: hex nexus start` and exits with code 1 (no panic, no raw mode left engaged)

specs.description: Streaming errors are displayed inline without crashing the TUI
specs.given: A streaming request that receives `data: {"error":"..."}`
specs.when: The error event is parsed by the TUI
specs.then: The error text appears in the assistant message area in red; the TUI remains interactive so the user can try again

specs.description: Pressing Enter on an empty input does nothing
specs.given: The input buffer is empty
specs.then: No API request is made; no empty user message appears in the conversation area

specs.description: Key input is ignored while a response is streaming
specs.given: An active streaming response is in progress
specs.when: User presses Enter or types characters
specs.then: No new request is sent; the input buffer accepts typed characters but Enter is a no-op until streaming completes

specs.description: Terminal raw mode is always restored on exit, even after a panic
specs.given: Any exit path — user quits with q/Ctrl+C, error occurs, or streaming is cancelled
specs.then: The terminal is restored to cooked mode and the alternate screen is left; no garbage ANSI codes remain in the shell

specs.description: Ollama streaming format is parsed correctly
specs.given: A registered Ollama provider returning NDJSON with `{"message":{"content":"tok"},"done":false}`
specs.when: The streaming endpoint receives the Ollama response
specs.then: Each NDJSON line is converted to `{"token":"tok"}` SSE events; the final `{"done":true}` line emits the completion event

specs.description: Provider selection falls back to OpenRouter when no registered providers match
specs.given: No registered providers; OPENROUTER_API_KEY is set in env
specs.when: POST /api/inference/chat/stream is called without a model override
specs.then: A synthetic OpenRouter endpoint using `openai/gpt-4o-mini` is selected and streaming proceeds normally

specs.description: Assistant responses render markdown — code blocks, bold, inline code, bullet lists
specs.given: An assistant message containing markdown: a fenced code block, **bold** text, `inline code`, and a bullet list
specs.when: The message is rendered in the TUI
specs.then: Code blocks appear with a visually distinct background and a language label; bold text is rendered with BOLD modifier; inline code is colored; bullet points use • glyphs — all without raw markdown syntax visible

specs.description: Conversation flows as a natural scrollback — no heavy window borders around messages
specs.given: A hex chat session with multiple turns
specs.then: Messages appear as a continuous flowing conversation: each turn shows a dimmed role label followed by the message content; there is no bordered 'Messages' panel; the layout feels like a terminal REPL, not a windowed app

specs.description: Shift+Enter inserts a newline into the input, Enter submits
specs.given: User is composing a multi-line message
specs.then: A newline is inserted into the input buffer and the input area expands to show all lines; pressing Enter (without Shift) submits the message

specs.description: Role labels are visually distinct and color-coded
specs.given: A conversation with both user and assistant messages
specs.then: User messages show a green 'you' label; assistant messages show a cyan 'hex' label; both are dimmed so content stands out; a thin horizontal rule separates each message turn

specs.description: Project context is injected into every session as a system prompt
specs.given: hex-nexus is running with a registered project and at least one ADR or workplan
specs.then: A system message is prepended to the conversation containing: project name, active workplan summaries, recent ADR titles, and available hex commands — the model can answer questions like 'what workplans are active' without the user providing context

specs.description: `/help` slash command displays available commands inline
specs.given: User is in a hex chat session
specs.then: A formatted list of slash commands and their descriptions appears in the message area; no inference request is sent

specs.description: `/clear` resets conversation history but keeps system context
specs.given: A session with multiple prior messages
specs.then: All messages are removed from the visible area and from the API messages array; the injected system context is preserved; a confirmation line appears

specs.description: `/model <name>` switches the active model for the session
specs.given: Session using model A
specs.then: Subsequent inference requests use the new model; the title bar updates to show the new model name

specs.description: `/adr <query>` searches ADRs and displays results inline
specs.given: hex-nexus has indexed ADRs
specs.then: Matching ADR titles, IDs, and status appear in the message area; no inference request is sent

specs.description: `/plan` lists active workplans with status
specs.given: At least one workplan exists
specs.then: A list of workplans with their phase/task completion counts appears inline; no inference request is sent

specs.description: Tool use: model can call hex_adr_search and receive results
specs.given: Session with a tool-capable model (OpenAI-compat with tools support)
specs.when: User asks 'find ADRs about authentication' and the model emits a tool_call for hex_adr_search
specs.then: The TUI shows a collapsed '⚙ hex_adr_search(...)' block; the tool executes via nexus; results are injected into the conversation; the model continues its response using the results

specs.description: Session is auto-saved after each completed turn
specs.given: A completed assistant response (done event received)
specs.then: The full conversation is written to `~/.hex/sessions/chat-{uuid}.json` with messages, model, timestamp, and project_id

specs.description: `hex chat --resume` shows a session picker and restores a prior session
specs.given: At least one saved session in `~/.hex/sessions/`
specs.then: An arrow-key selection list of recent sessions (timestamp + first user message preview) appears; selecting one restores the full conversation history and continues the session

specs.description: Unknown slash commands show an error inline without sending to the model
specs.given: User types `/unknown`
specs.then: An inline error 'Unknown command: /unknown — type /help for available commands' appears; no inference request is made
